name: Alabasta Kingdom
description: Alabasta is a massive desert kingdom in Paradise, one of the twenty founding nations of the World Government. The kingdom is characterized by vast deserts, ancient ruins, and cities built around oases. For three years prior to the Straw Hats' arrival, the kingdom suffered from a severe drought that only affected inland areas while the capital received rain - a conspiracy orchestrated by Crocodile using Dance Powder. This created a civil war between the king's forces and rebels who believed King Cobra was hoarding rain. The kingdom holds one of the three Ancient Weapons, Pluton, whose location is recorded on a Poneglyph hidden in the royal tomb.
history: Alabasta has stood for over 4,000 years, ruled by the Nefertari family who were one of the twenty royal families that formed the World Government 800 years ago. Uniquely, the Nefertaris chose to remain in Alabasta rather than move to Mary Geoise, serving as one of the few royal families still ruling their original kingdom. The kingdom prospered through trade routes and oases management until Crocodile, one of the Seven Warlords of the Sea, arrived and established himself as a hero while secretly orchestrating its downfall through his criminal organization, Baroque Works. Using Dance Powder to steal rain from inland cities and frame the king, Crocodile incited a civil war as cover for his true goal: obtaining the ancient weapon Pluton. The Straw Hats, allied with Princess Vivi, exposed Crocodile's conspiracy, defeated Baroque Works, and stopped the rebellion, though not before a massive battle in Alubarna. Crocodile was defeated by Luffy and imprisoned, and the kingdom began its recovery.
culture: Alabasta's culture is deeply tied to desert survival and water conservation. Cities are built around oases, and water management is sacred. The people are resilient and proud, with strong martial traditions exemplified by the royal guard's Zoan Devil Fruit users. The kingdom values loyalty to the crown, though the drought and conspiracy created unprecedented division. Traditional architecture features sandstone buildings designed to stay cool, and the Super Spot-Billed Duck is the primary mode of transportation. The kingdom has a rich history of ancient civilization, with ruins and tombs scattered throughout the desert that predate even the current dynasty.
transportation: Super Spot-Billed Ducks are the primary transportation, capable of running at incredible speeds across the desert. Ships dock at port cities like Nanohana and Tamarisk. The Sandora River connects some cities. Moving Crab vehicles are used for desert crossings. The vast distances between oases make travel dangerous without proper preparation.

traditions: Shared meals as sign of trust
Festival foods when water is abundant
quirks: Water more valuable than food during drought
Ukkari Hot-Spicy Perfume mistaken for food

notablePeople.name: Sir Crocodile (Mr. 0)
notablePeople.role: Baroque Works Leader / Former Warlord of the Sea
notablePeople.description: A Logia-type Devil Fruit user with the Sand-Sand Fruit, making him immune to physical attacks and able to control sand. Orchestrated Alabasta's civil war to obtain Pluton. Defeated by Luffy and sent to Impel Down.

notablePeople.name: Nefertari Vivi
notablePeople.description: The beloved princess who infiltrated Baroque Works to save her kingdom. Traveled with the Straw Hats and formed deep bonds with them. Chose to stay in Alabasta to help rebuild after the war.

notablePeople.name: Nefertari Cobra
notablePeople.description: The wise and benevolent 12th king of Alabasta. Falsely accused of hoarding rain, he maintained faith that the truth would emerge. Trusted Vivi's judgment about the Straw Hats.

notablePeople.name: Nico Robin (Miss All Sunday)
notablePeople.description: Crocodile's partner and the only person who can read Poneglyphs. After Crocodile's defeat, she forced her way onto the Straw Hat crew, beginning her journey to discover the true history.

notablePeople.name: Kohza
notablePeople.description: Vivi's childhood friend who leads the rebel army against the king. Once realized the truth about the drought conspiracy, he immediately worked to stop the war.

notablePeople.name: Pell the Falcon
notablePeople.description: One of the two strongest warriors in Alabasta, possessing the rare Bird-Bird Fruit Model: Falcon. Sacrificed himself to carry a bomb away from Alubarna, miraculously surviving.

notablePeople.name: Chaka the Jackal
notablePeople.description: One of the two strongest warriors in Alabasta, with the Dog-Dog Fruit Model: Jackal. Head of the royal guard and fiercely loyal to the Nefertari family.

notablePeople.name: Mr. 1 (Daz Bonez)
notablePeople.description: Crocodile's top male officer agent with the Dice-Dice Fruit, turning his body into blades. Fought Zoro in a battle that awakened Zoro's ability to cut steel.

notablePeople.name: Miss Doublefinger (Zala)
notablePeople.description: Mr. 1's partner with the Spike-Spike Fruit, able to grow spikes from any part of her body. Fought Nami in the palace.

notablePeople.name: Mr. 2 Bon Clay (Bentham)
notablePeople.description: An okama martial artist with the Clone-Clone Fruit, able to perfectly copy anyone's appearance. Became friends with the Straw Hats despite being enemies, showing a complex sense of honor.

notablePeople.name: Mr. 3 (Galdino)
notablePeople.description: The wax-manipulating officer from Little Garden who participated in the final battles at Alubarna.

notablePeople.name: Mr. 4
notablePeople.description: A slow but powerful officer who wields a bazooka that 'ate' the Dog-Dog Fruit Model: Dachshund. Partnered with Miss Merry Christmas.

notablePeople.name: Miss Merry Christmas (Drophy)
notablePeople.description: An elderly woman with the Mole-Mole Fruit who can tunnel underground. Partnered with Mr. 4 and fought Usopp and Chopper.

notablePeople.name: Portgas D. Ace
notablePeople.description: Luffy's older brother who appeared in Alabasta searching for Blackbeard. Possesses the Flame-Flame Fruit. Saved Luffy from Smoker and shared a meal before departing.

notablePeople.name: Smoker (White Hunter)
notablePeople.description: The Marine pursuing the Straw Hats from Loguetown. Possesses the Smoke-Smoke Fruit. Began to question absolute justice after witnessing the Straw Hats save Alabasta while the World Government covered up the truth.

notablePeople.name: Tashigi
notablePeople.description: Smoker's subordinate and skilled swordswoman who resembles Zoro's deceased friend Kuina. Conflicted about pirates after seeing the Straw Hats' heroism.

subLocations.name: Alubarna
subLocations.description: The capital city and seat of the royal palace. Built around a massive oasis, it's the only city that continued receiving rain during the drought. The final battle between Baroque Works and the Straw Hats took place here.

subLocations.name: Rainbase
subLocations.description: A casino city in the desert that served as Crocodile's base of operations. Home to the Rain Dinners casino, which housed his secret underground headquarters.

subLocations.name: Nanohana
subLocations.description: A coastal port city famous for perfume production. Where the Straw Hats first landed in Alabasta and had their first encounter with Smoker in the kingdom.

subLocations.name: Yuba
subLocations.description: Once a thriving oasis city, now reduced to a desert wasteland by the drought. Home to the rebel army's base and Kohza's father Toto, who never stopped digging for water.

subLocations.name: Katorea
subLocations.description: A city on the Sandora River where a massive battle between the royal army and rebel forces took place. Mr. 2 Bon Clay impersonated the king here to incite further violence.

subLocations.name: Sandora Desert
subLocations.description: The vast desert that covers most of Alabasta. Extremely dangerous with scorching heat, sandstorms, and desert creatures. Contains ancient ruins and the entry to the royal tomb.

subLocations.name: Sandora River
subLocations.description: The great river that runs through Alabasta, vital to the kingdom's survival. Several cities are built along its banks.

subLocations.name: Royal Tomb
subLocations.description: The underground tomb where Alabasta's kings are buried. Contains the Poneglyph that reveals Pluton's location. Where Luffy had his final showdown with Crocodile.

items.name: Pluton Poneglyph
items.description: The Poneglyph hidden in Alabasta's royal tomb that reveals the location of Pluton, one of the three Ancient Weapons capable of mass destruction. Crocodile's ultimate goal.

items.name: Dance Powder
items.description: A banned powder that creates artificial rain by stealing moisture from other areas. Used by Crocodile to create the drought and frame King Cobra, inciting the civil war.

items.name: Eternal Pose to Alabasta
items.description: The permanent Log Pose given to the Straw Hats at Whiskey Peak to guide them to Alabasta.

items.name: Crocodile's Hook
items.description: Crocodile's golden hook that contains a hidden poison blade. His signature weapon used in combat.

items.name: Time Bomb
items.description: A massive bomb with a 5-kilometer blast radius planted in Alubarna's clock tower. Pell sacrificed himself to carry it away from the city.

items.name: Clima-Tact
items.description: The weather-manipulating weapon created by Usopp for Nami, first used effectively in battle against Miss Doublefinger.

items.name: Mera Mera no Mi Powers
items.description: Ace's Flame-Flame Logia fruit that allows him to create, control, and become fire.

items.name: Alabasta Perfume
items.description: Famous perfume produced in Nanohana, a major export of the kingdom. Ukkari Hot-Spicy Perfume played a role in the story.

items.name: Kung-Fu Dugong Shells
items.description: Shells marking Luffy's defeat of the Kung-Fu Dugongs, who then became his 'students' in a comedic subplot.

factions.name: Baroque Works
factions.description: The criminal syndicate orchestrating Alabasta's civil war to obtain Pluton. Defeated and dismantled by the Straw Hats.

factions.name: Alabasta Royal Family
factions.description: The ruling family of Alabasta, descended from one of the twenty founding families of the World Government. Chose to remain in Alabasta 800 years ago.

factions.name: Rebel Army
members: Thousands of citizens from drought-stricken cities
factions.description: Citizens who rebelled against the king believing he was hoarding rain. Stood down after learning the truth about Crocodile's conspiracy.

factions.name: Straw Hat Pirates (Allies)
factions.description: The pirate crew that allied with Vivi to save Alabasta, defeated Baroque Works, and stopped the civil war.

factions.name: Marines (Alabasta Unit)
factions.description: The Marine forces pursuing the Straw Hats who arrived in Alabasta. Witnessed the truth but were ordered to credit the World Government for Crocodile's defeat.

factions.name: Whitebeard Pirates (Visitors)
factions.description: Representatives of one of the Four Emperors' crews. Ace visited Alabasta hunting Blackbeard and briefly reunited with Luffy.

events: Learning about the civil war and drought
Captured by Crocodile at Rain Dinners
Race across the desert to Alubarna
Straw Hats vs Baroque Works officer battles
World Government covers up the truth
Straw Hat farewell (X mark on arms)

quickFacts: One of the twenty founding kingdoms of World Government
Home to Ancient Weapon Pluton (location hidden)
3-year drought orchestrated by Crocodile using Dance Powder
Civil war between rebels and royal army
Where Luffy defeated his first Warlord of the Sea
Nico Robin joins the Straw Hat crew here
Vivi's emotional farewell to the Straw Hats

videos.title: Luffy vs Crocodile Final Battle
videos.description: Luffy's climactic third battle against Crocodile in the royal tomb, defeating the Warlord with water-enhanced attacks.

videos.title: Vivi's Farewell - X Marks
videos.description: The emotional goodbye where the Straw Hats and Vivi silently show their bond with the X marks on their arms.

videos.title: Pell's Sacrifice
videos.description: Pell carries the massive bomb away from Alubarna to save the kingdom.
